announcements.title: Password Vault v2.0 — AES-256 Upgrade & New Bin Feature
announcements.author: Shyam Sunder Kanth
announcements.excerpt: Password Vault 2.0 ships with AES-256 encryption, a new deleted-passwords bin, and improved backup/restore via SAF.

content.text: Password Vault 2.0 is a major update focused on security hardening and usability improvements. After months of feedback from early users, we've addressed the most-requested features and patched several edge cases.

items: Upgraded encryption from AES-128 to AES-256 for stronger local security
New Bin feature — deleted passwords are recoverable for 30 days
Improved backup & restore using Android Storage Access Framework (SAF)
Password strength meter now shows entropy score
Minor UI polish across the vault list and detail screens

content.text: Existing vaults will be automatically re-encrypted on first launch after the update. This is a one-time process and may take a few seconds depending on the number of stored entries.
All your data stays on-device. No cloud, no telemetry, no surprises.
content.author: Shyam Sunder Kanth

content.text: The update is live on the Google Play Store. Search for Password Vault or use the link in the Projects section of the portfolio.

announcements.title: Japnaam is Now Live on Google Play
announcements.author: Shyam Sunder Kanth
announcements.excerpt: Japnaam — the offline spiritual companion for tracking jaap counts, aartis, and chalisas — is officially available on the Play Store.

content.text: Japnaam is a simple, distraction-free app built for people who want to keep a daily spiritual practice. It works fully offline and stores everything locally on your device.

items: Track jaap counts for multiple mantras
Browse a curated collection of aartis and chalisas
All data stored locally — no account required
Clean, minimal UI with no ads

content.text:
// Built with
Kotlin + MVVM
Room Database
Jetpack Compose
Coroutines + Flow
Japnaam is available for free on Google Play. No in-app purchases, no subscriptions.

announcements.title: Privacy Policy Section Added to Portfolio
announcements.author: Shyam Sunder Kanth
announcements.excerpt: The portfolio now includes a dedicated Privacy Policies section covering all published apps — fully JSON-driven and easy to maintain.

content.text: Google Play requires a publicly accessible privacy policy for every app. Instead of hosting separate static pages, I built a unified, JSON-driven privacy policy system directly into the portfolio.

items: All policies live in a single policy-data.json file
Adding a new policy = adding one JSON object, zero HTML changes
Listing page auto-generates cards for every policy
Detail page renders sections, subsections, lists, and contact blocks dynamically
Fully responsive and matches the portfolio's dark theme

content.text: You can find it under the 'More' menu in the portfolio navigation, or directly at /privacy-policy/.

announcements.title: PayDay EMI Reminder App — In Development
announcements.author: Shyam Sunder Kanth
announcements.excerpt: PayDay, an Android app for tracking EMIs and payment reminders, is currently under active development.

content.text: PayDay is an upcoming Android application designed to help users track their EMIs, loan repayments, and recurring payment deadlines. The goal is a simple, local-first app with smart notifications.

items: Add and manage multiple EMIs with due dates
Local push notifications before payment dates
Monthly summary dashboard
No login, no cloud — fully offline
Export to CSV

content.text:
// Planned tech stack
Kotlin + Hilt
Room + DataStore
MVVM + Clean Architecture
WorkManager (notifications)
Jetpack Compose
Expected release: Q1 2026. Follow the GitHub profile for updates.

announcements.title: Lumina Habit — A Beautiful Static Habit Tracker Built with Vanilla JS
announcements.author: Shyam Sunder Kanth
announcements.excerpt: Lumina Habit is a fully static, localStorage-powered habit tracking web app with a dark green aesthetic, session logging, journaling, analytics, and data import/export — no backend required.

content.text: Lumina Habit is a fully client-side habit tracking web app built with vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. There is no backend, no database, and no login — everything lives in your browser's localStorage. It was designed to feel premium while staying completely offline-capable.

items: Create and manage habits with custom icons, colors, and unit types
Daily check-in system with streak tracking and completion stats
Session logging with duration, intensity, and reflection notes
GitHub-style consistency heatmap across the past year
Daily journal with mood tagging and timestamped entries
Analytics page with weekly bar chart, activity rhythm, and peak time insight
Full data export and import via JSON backup files
Activity log that records every action taken in the app
Responsive design with mobile bottom navigation and desktop nav
Profile setup with name persistence across all pages

content.text: The app is structured as a multi-page static site with a shared global script handling the data store, activity logging, toast notifications, and navigation. Each page — Dashboard, Habits, Stats, and Settings — has its own HTML, CSS, and JS file. Styling is done with Tailwind CSS via CDN, and all state is persisted through a structured localStorage API.
No server. No cloud. No excuses. Just you and your habits.
content.author: Shyam Sunder Kanth

content.text: Unlike most habit trackers that only support building positive habits, Lumina Habit includes dedicated unit types for avoidance habits — tracking days cigarette-free, days alcohol-free, urges resisted, and more. The unit catalogue spans seven categories covering time, volume, distance, exercise, content, avoidance, and completion.

Open command pallete pressing CTRL + /  or on phone touch and hold screen three fingers for five seciond and select Lumina from the options available.  Also Lumina Habit is available in the Projects section of the portfolio. Open index.html in any browser — no build step, no dependencies to install.

announcements.title: Botbook v2 — Redefining Minimalist Note-Taking with Data Sovereignty
announcements.author: Shyam Sunder Kanth
announcements.excerpt: Botbook v2 emerges with a refined, high-contrast minimalist UI, focused entirely on speed and portability. Now featuring full JSON-based import/export for total control over your digital thoughts.

content.text: Botbook v2 is the successor to our original lightweight note-taking tool. This version strips away the noise to focus on a 'write-first' philosophy. Built with an obsession for typography and white space, it transforms the browser into a focused sanctuary for your ideas, utilizing advanced CSS grid systems and a refined dark-mode palette.

items: Ultra-minimalist interface with a focus on typography and readability
Instant-save architecture using a optimized localStorage engine
New: 'Export All' feature to download your entire library as a single, portable JSON file
New: 'Import Notes' engine to restore backups or merge notes across different devices
Categorized 'Notebooks' with dynamic color-coding for visual organization
Global search functionality that filters through content in real-time as you type
Markdown support for rich-text formatting without the heavy overhead
Reading time estimates and word count tracking for long-form entries
Auto-backup prompts to ensure your local data is never lost
Fully responsive layout optimized for seamless writing on mobile or desktop

content.text: The interface leverages a monochrome-plus-one accent system, ensuring that the UI never distracts from the content. By utilizing modern CSS features like backdrop-filters and custom properties, Botbook v2 achieves a 'glass-morphism' aesthetic that feels integrated into the modern web while maintaining a lightning-fast performance profile.
Your notes shouldn't be trapped in a cloud. They should be in your pocket, in your control, and in your style.
content.author: Shyam Sunder Kanth

content.text: The standout feature of v2 is the Import/Export suite. Recognizing that users need to move between browsers and devices, you can now generate a full encrypted backup of your notes library. Simply drag and drop your exported JSON file into any new instance of Botbook to pick up exactly where you left off.

Botbook v2 is live within the local ecosystem. Access it via the global command palette (CTRL + /) or navigate to the Projects section of the portfolio. Just like Lumina, it is a single-entry point application—open the index.html and start writing immediately.

announcements.title: Lexica — A Private Linguistic Vault for Your Personal Vocabulary
announcements.author: Shyam Sunder Kanth
announcements.excerpt: Lexica is a minimalist, localStorage-powered personal dictionary designed for writers, students, and language lovers to curate their own collection of words, definitions, pronunciations, and contextual examples.

content.text: Lexica is a fully client-side web application that serves as a private vault for your linguistic discoveries. It combines a clean, academic aesthetic with powerful organizational tools, allowing users to build a custom lexicon without any external dependencies, accounts, or cloud storage. It is designed for those who find beauty in words and want a dedicated space to archive them.

items: Create a personal lexicon with word definitions, pronunciations, and example sentences
Categorize entries by parts of speech (Noun, Verb, Adjective, Adverb) or custom categories
Dashboard with real-time stats: Total Words, Weekly Growth, and Favorite count
Automated 'Word of the Day' feature that resurfaces curated artifacts from your library
Advanced search interface with multi-criteria filtering and sorting
Star/Favorite system for highlighting your most cherished linguistic findings
Live Activity Log that records every action, from additions to data exports
Full data portability with JSON-based import and export capabilities
Responsive 'App-like' design with mobile bottom-nav and desktop sidebar layouts
Customizable profile section to track your journey as a Lexicographer

content.text: Lexica is built using a modern multi-page architecture with Vanilla HTML5, CSS3, and JavaScript. Unlike frameworks that rely on heavy dependencies, Lexica uses a custom-built localStorage API for state persistence and Material Symbols for its minimalist iconography. The styling focuses on a refined dark-themed aesthetic with deep charcoal tones and a focus on typography.

To encourage engagement with your collected vocabulary, Lexica features a 'Word of the Day' system. It randomly selects an entry from your database and presents it in a beautiful curated format, helping you stay connected with words you've archived. The app also includes dedicated fields for pronunciation and etymological notes, making it a comprehensive tool for serious word collectors.

Open the command palette by pressing CTRL + / or by using the global navigation on the portfolio. Lexica is a zero-setup application — simply navigate to the lexicon and start adding words. All your data is saved instantly to your browser and can be backed up as a JSON file at any time.
